message:
fix(fnp-python): percentile/quantile empty input raises IndexError (numpy parity)

Our in-Rust percentile/quantile paths returned Ok(NaN) on empty input,
skipping the on-error fallback and handing NaN back to the caller.
numpy.percentile / numpy.quantile raise IndexError on empty input
(internal kth-element lookup into a 0-length array). User code catching
IndexError specifically was missing our silent-NaN return.

Fix: check a.values().is_empty() before the in-Rust call and fall back
to numpy so the IndexError type, message, and traceback match exactly.
The non-empty fast path is unchanged.

The percentile/quantile empty-input probe (5f811e9) now consistently
passes under parallel cargo test.
